ops.title: ccx secret masking: design boundaries and vendored rules
ops.body:
# ccx secret masking (v0.19.0) — deliberate boundaries

Born from the AwardWallet key leak (session d6e3c5dc: a guard whole-line rewrite turned a delete-chain into `ccx code read .env --full`, printing a live key). The rewrite class is fixed (v0.18.0 per-occurrence); masking is defense-in-depth on the read lane. Four decisions to not "fix" piecemeal:

**Engine: vendored rules, not the gitleaks library.** `internal/secrets/gitleaks.toml` is gitleaks v8.30.1's default config (MIT), evaluated with stdlib `regexp` (same RE2 syntax) + a gitleaks-faithful Shannon entropy check (counts runes, normalizes by BYTE length — pinned by a non-ASCII test row). Importing `detect`/`config` instead would drag viper + wasilibs/go-re2 (~3.2MB WASM) for no detection gain; module path upstream is still `zricethezav` (repo moved orgs, go.mod never followed). Skipped fidelity, deliberately: allowlists/stopwords, `required` proximity chaining, base64/hex codec re-decoding — skipping only over-masks or under-fires on the generic rule, never leaks a provider-rule secret. Refresh = re-vendor the TOML from a newer tag; the rules-compile test gates every kept rule and pins counts.

**Scope: `generic-api-key` fires only on env-shaped paths** (`.env`, `.env.*`, `*.env`, `.envrc`, `credentials`, `.netrc`). A high-entropy secret hardcoded in `.yaml`/`.json`/`.toml`/source is NOT masked — confirmed leak path, accepted: the rule is documented-noisy on ordinary source (gitleaks #1578, #1830, #1775) and unscoped it repaints lockfile hashes and fixture blobs, gutting read fidelity for the tool's main job. Provider-prefix rules (AKIA…, ghp_…, private-key PEM, …) apply to every path. Widen envShaped only with FP evidence in hand.

**Surface: OpRead only.** grep/outline/symbol/diff/show emit raw content — backlogged as task 8a103e8, per-surface choke points differ (diff never reaches Finalize).

**Escape hatch is visible.** `--reveal-secrets`/`reveal_secrets` prints raw AND forfeits the guard pack's read auto-approval (approval_guards carve-out, pack 0.7.0) — the human sees a permission prompt before an agent unmasks. Masking runs BEFORE render.Cap (the exec lane calls with Budget==0 where Cap is a no-op — Cap-side masking would be a bypass); the mask footer appends after Cap so it survives budget cuts. Whole-match spans trim trailing newline/delimiter bytes so the read never reflows lines or eats a closing quote; spans <16 bytes mask fully (no 4-byte stub).
ops.when: Before changing ccx secret-masking policy, adjusting envShaped scoping, updating the vendored gitleaks rules, or touching render.Finalize's OpRead branch
